title: epipactis tremolsii subsp. lapidocampi
intro: Epipactis tremolsii subsp. lapidocampi, also known as the limestone helleborine, is a rare and protected subspecies of orchid native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, with potential benefits for the treatment of various health conditions, including respiratory issues, skin irritations, and digestive problems. The medicinal properties of Epipactis tremolsii subsp. lapidocampi can be harnessed through herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, although the optimal methods and dosages require further research. Further studies are needed to fully understand the potential health benefits and optimal uses of this rare and protected plant species.
